basicInfo.name: Nightmare Zhao Yun
basicInfo.flavor: When Guan Yu was elevated to the title of King Muan, Zhao Yun was likewise elevated to the title of Marquis Shunping. But the common people insisted on calling him \"King Shunping\" rather than \"Marquis\" as a sign of their respect.

Normal Attack.desc:
Single Enemy
 - Deals damage equal to 100% of Physical Attack 1 time(s).
 - 100% chance to inflict Bleed [Duration: 10 Turn(s)]
Skill Enhancement Effect
 - Increases Damage: To 100% of Physical Attack.

Skill 1.name: Tornado Thrust
Skill 1.desc:
Single Enemy
 - Deals damage equal to 285% of Physical Attack 1 time(s).
 - 100% chance to inflict Bleed [Duration: 10 Turn(s)]
 - Deals additional damage equal to 1500% of Physical Attack against targets inflicted with [Bleed].
Skill Enhancement Effect
 - Increases Damage: To 285% of Physical Attack.

Skill 2.name: Splitting Tornado Thrust
Skill 2.desc:
All Enemies
 - Deals damage equal to 90% of Physical Attack 1 time(s).
 - 100% chance to inflict Bleed [Duration: 10 Turn(s)]
 - Deals additional damage equal to 1500% of Physical Attack against targets inflicted with [Bleed].
Skill Enhancement Effect
 - Increases Damage: To 90% of Physical Attack.

Passive.name: Protective Aura
Passive.desc:
Self
 - Grants All Damage Immunity. [Duration: 7 Turn(s)]

profile.story:
During the War of Destruction, Zhao Yun's hometown was plagued by roving bandits.
Though Zhao Yun was still young, he was able to single-handedly wipe out the bandits using his exceptional martial arts skills.
But upon returning home, he discovered it had already been annihilated by a different group of bandits.
Zhao Yun left his decimated hometown and wandered the lands until one day he came across Guan Yu leading his soldiers into battle for the sake of the common people.
That area was ravaged by bandits as well, but Guan Yu divided his troops into two forces: one to establish an impenetrable defense perimeter around the village, and another he personally led on a surprise attack on the bandit stronghold, defeating them without any casualties.
When he witnessed this, Zhao Yun came to the painful realization that no matter how strong he was individually, there was only so much he could accomplish on his own.
He saw in Guan Yu the noble character and leadership qualities he lacked in himself, and entered Guan Yu's service.
Later on, Zhao Yun also became a brilliant general in his own right. Like Guan Yu, he possessed a strong sense of justice and earned widespread respect and recognition from the populace, eventually earning the moniker of King Shunping, meaning "King of Harmonious Peace."
Though it was not an official title bestowed by the Emperor, the fact that he was called a king is testament to how revered he was by the people.
